que: In Flow, what is the primary goal?
options: emit asynchronous streams over time
replace testing entirely
skip state/data modeling
avoid understanding requirements

que: Which API/concept is most central to Flow?
options: manual polling loop for all tasks
legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic
random utility class name
Flow

que: What is a recommended best practice for Flow?
options: mix unrelated responsibilities in one function
collect emissions in a coroutine
ignore edge/error states
optimize before verifying correctness

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing Flow?
options: follow official docs for API usage
write focused tests for behavior
keep boundaries clear
blocking thread while waiting for updates

que: For production code, which approach fits Flow best?
options: Use Flow only as theory with no implementation
Use Flow with clear state flow and testable logic
Use Flow while bypassing architecture boundaries
Use Flow and suppress all warnings

que: A teammate asks where to start with Flow. What is best advice?
options: avoid feedback from tests/review
Read official docs (https://developer.android.com/) and implement a small sample
copy an unknown snippet without context
skip running the app after changes

que: Which statement about Flow is most accurate?
options: Flow should be applied identically in every layer
Flow is mainly about adding boilerplate
Flow is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data
Flow removes the need for any testing

que: In a code review for Flow, what would you flag?
options: Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly
explicit modeling of UI/data states
small pure functions with clear names
traceable data flow between layers

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using Flow correctly?
options: A small, working feature that demonstrates Flow in context
a large refactor without tests
a slide deck with no runnable code
an implementation with no error states

que: To learn Flow deeply, what should you do next?
options: memorize definitions only
Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using Flow patterns
skip verification to save time
avoid official references
